title: Kernel subagent6 glm-recovery lane. Investigate only why local GLM wrapper calls would fail with fetch failed in this repo/session, and suggest one bounded recovery attempt that does not require changing repository code. Do not run commands; provide likely cause and one safe retry pattern.
summary_text: Most likely cause in this session: outbound network egress to the GLM API is blocked or unstable at the session/sandbox layer, so the local wrapper’s Node `fetch` throws the generic `fetch failed` before any repo logic is involved. One bounded, safe recovery attempt (no repo code changes): 1. Re-run the same GLM wrapper call in a clean shell context with only required GLM env vars (`API key`, `base URL`) and a hard timeout. 2. If it fails with `fetch failed`, wait 5-10 seconds and retry exactly once with the same inputs. 3. If the second attempt fails, stop (no further retries) and treat it as infra/session connectivity, not a code defect. Workflow guard: avoid broad single-lane fallback behavior beyond this single bounded retry, since that would be a Kernel orchestration policy violation.
